Source   Edit  

Espionage Action Descriptors

Data-oriented design: Extract action-specific data from execution logic This eliminates 93% code duplication in engine.nim (448 lines → ~30 lines)

Action-Specific DataAction Descriptor Table

Imports

types, ../../common/types/core, ../config/prestige_config, ../config/espionage_config

Types

ActionDescriptor = object
  action*: EspionageAction
  detectedDesc*: string
  successDesc*: string
  failedPrestigeReason*: string
  successPrestigeReason*: string
  attackerSuccessPrestige*: int
  targetSuccessPrestige*: int
  targetSuccessReason*: string
  requiresSystem*: bool
  hasEffect*: bool
  effectType*: EffectType
  effectTurns*: int
  effectMagnitude*: float
  effectTargetsSelf*: bool
  stealsSRP*: bool
  srpAmount*: int
  damagesIU*: bool
  damageDice*: int
  stealsIntel*: bool
Data describing an espionage action's effects Source   Edit  

Procs

proc getActionDescriptor(action: EspionageAction): ActionDescriptor {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get action-specific data for execution Pure function - all action mechanics defined here Source   Edit  

Exports

CIP_COST_PP, DISINFORMATION_DURATION, DetectionAttempt, DISINFORMATION_MIN_VARIANCE, PrestigeEvent, PLANT_DISINFORMATION_COST, INVESTMENT_THRESHOLD, SABOTAGE_LOW_DICE, COUNTER_INTEL_SWEEP_COST, EspionageAction, HouseId, TECH_THEFT_SRP, TECH_THEFT_COST, EspionageBudget, calculateOverInvestmentPenalty, EFFECT_DURATION, ECONOMIC_MANIPULATION_COST, EspionageResult, SABOTAGE_HIGH_COST, ASSASSINATION_COST, getCIPModifier, SystemId, FAILED_ESPIONAGE_PENALTY, DISINFORMATION_MAX_VARIANCE, INVESTMENT_PENALTY, DetectionResult, EBP_COST_PP, CIP_DEDUCTION_PER_ROLL, EffectType, SABOTAGE_LOW_COST, EspionageReport, OngoingEffect, SABOTAGE_HIGH_DICE, INTELLIGENCE_THEFT_COST, PSYOPS_CAMPAIGN_COST, CYBER_ATTACK_COST, PSYOPS_REDUCTION, ASSASSINATION_REDUCTION, globalEspionageConfig, getActionCost, INTEL_BLOCK_DURATION, EspionageAttempt, getDetectionThreshold, initEspionageBudget, CICLevel, ECONOMIC_REDUCTION
